mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with rate changes.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0 percent, consumer spending power deteriorates rapidly, triggering earnings downgrades and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in over 55 percent of instances where the unemployment threshold is breached, or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio falls below zero.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in existing home sales and new housing starts signal strengthening consumer balance sheets and rising construction activity, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark by at least 100 basis points over 20-day horizons in backtests, or correlation drops below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month expansion in the index of industrial production indicates recovering factory utilization and supply chain normalization, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Signal accuracy drops below 45 percent during early recovery periods, or 5-day forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero across multiple recovery cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising wage income, which supports corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains negative over any 24-month rolling window, or payroll growth shows zero predictive power for 20-day equity direction.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index annualized growth exceeds 3.0 percent, real discount rates rise and central bank tightening expectations accelerate, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally by more than 2 percent in 60 days following inflation threshold breaches in over 60 percent of historical cases.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent contraction in real GDP and rising unemployment signals a deepening demand shock that forces corporate earnings revisions downward, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces positive 20-day returns in more than half of triggered observations, or interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a liquidity crisis reflects severe credit constraints and inventory drawdowns, which amplifies market panic and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Signal fails to predict negative returns during documented financial crises, or 5-day forward returns remain positive in over 50 percent of crisis-period observations.
